turns.text:
Happy to help spec this out. A few questions:
1. What should netfetch's timeout flag be called, and what unit should the value be in?
2. What should happen to the existing default behavior if --timeout isn't passed?
3. What exit code should a timed-out download use?
4. Any constraints on adding new dependencies for the implementation?

Call it --timeout, whole seconds. Keep the default at 30 seconds if it's not passed. Use exit code 124, same as the Unix timeout command -- our CI already keys retries off that. And no new npm dependencies at all, netfetch has to stay dependency-free.

Got it. A couple more:
1. What is netfetch actually used for, so I get the framing right in the spec?
2. Should there be a short alias like -t for --timeout?
3. If a timeout does fire, what should the error message include?

netfetch is our internal Node CLI for downloading build artifacts. A -t alias would be nice to have, not required. And yeah, if it times out the error should say which URL timed out -- that's saved me a lot of debugging time elsewhere. Oh, and a --retries flag to auto-retry would also be nice someday, though that's not needed for this release.

Here's the spec, written to docs/specs/timeout-flag.md. It covers: --timeout flag in whole seconds, default 30s preserved, exit code 124 on timeout, -t alias as a stretch goal, --retries as a stretch goal, error messages naming the timed-out URL, and zero new dependencies. Let me know if that looks right.
